question: Who built an altar and called it 'The-LORD-Is-My-Banner'?
explanation: Exodus 17:15 — Moses built an altar and called it The-LORD-Is-My-Banner (Jehovah Nissi).

question: Which tribe did Joshua belong to?
explanation: Numbers 13:8 — Joshua son of Nun was from the tribe of Ephraim.

question: What happened to Achan after he took forbidden items from Jericho?
explanation: Joshua 7:25 — Achan and all that he had were stoned with stones and burned with fire.

question: Who was the judge who made a rash vow about his daughter?
explanation: Judges 11:30-31 — Jephthah made a vow to the LORD concerning what came out of his house.

question: What was the name of Samson's Philistine wife who betrayed him?
explanation: Judges 16:4-19 — Delilah, whom Samson loved, discovered the secret of his strength and betrayed him.

question: What was the source of Samson's strength?
explanation: Judges 16:17 — A razor had never come upon Samson's head; if shaved, his strength would leave.

question: What did Orpah do when Naomi told her daughters-in-law to return home?
optionB: She wept and then returned to her people
explanation: Ruth 1:14 — Orpah kissed her mother-in-law and returned to her people, while Ruth clung to Naomi.

question: In which field did Ruth glean barley?
explanation: Ruth 2:3 — Ruth happened to come to the part of the field belonging to Boaz.

question: What was the name of Esther's Hebrew name?
explanation: Esther 2:7 — Mordecai had brought up Hadassah (that is, Esther), his uncle's daughter.

question: Who was King Ahasuerus's first queen whom he dethroned?
explanation: Esther 1:9-19 — Queen Vashti refused to come before the king and was dethroned.

question: Who was Eli in 1 Samuel?
explanation: 1 Samuel 1:9 — Eli the priest was sitting on his seat by the doorpost of the tabernacle of the LORD.

question: What happened to the Ark of God when it was captured by the Philistines?
optionB: Their god Dagon fell and broke
explanation: 1 Samuel 5:3-4 — Dagon had fallen on his face before the Ark, and his head and hands were cut off.

question: What was the sign by which Saul knew God had confirmed his kingship?
optionB: The Spirit of God came upon him
explanation: 1 Samuel 10:10 — The Spirit of God came upon Saul and he prophesied among the prophets.

question: Who was Abner in the books of Samuel?
explanation: 1 Samuel 14:50 — Abner was the son of Ner, Saul's uncle, and commander of his army.

question: Who were the Amalekites that Saul failed to completely destroy?
optionC: A nomadic enemy tribe of Israel
explanation: 1 Samuel 15 — God commanded Saul to destroy the Amalekites utterly, but Saul spared King Agag and the best animals.

question: What was the name of Saul's daughter who became David's wife?
explanation: 1 Samuel 18:27 — Saul gave him Michal his daughter as wife.

question: Who was the Philistine giant killed by Elhanan in 2 Samuel?
explanation: 2 Samuel 21:19 — Elhanan the son of Jaare-Oregim killed Lahmi the brother of Goliath.

question: Where did David bring the Ark of the LORD after becoming king?
explanation: 2 Samuel 6:12 — David brought up the Ark of God from the house of Obed-Edom to the City of David (Jerusalem).

question: In 2 Samuel 7, what did God promise David through the prophet Nathan?
optionB: A dynasty — that his throne would be established forever
optionC: Victory over all his enemies immediately
optionD: That he would build the temple
explanation: 2 Samuel 7:12-16 — God promised David an everlasting dynasty; his offspring would rule forever.

question: What did David do with Mephibosheth, Jonathan's son?
optionB: Restored to him all of Saul's land and gave him a place at the royal table
explanation: 2 Samuel 9:7 — David restored all of Saul's land to Mephibosheth and gave him a permanent seat at his table.

question: What is the Sermon on the Mount, and where does it appear?
optionB: Jesus's first recorded extended teaching — Matthew 5-7
optionC: A discourse on the end times — Matthew 24
optionD: A teaching given in the temple — John 7
explanation: Matthew 5-7 contains the Sermon on the Mount, Jesus's foundational ethical and spiritual teaching.

question: What are the Beatitudes?
optionB: Blessings declared by Jesus at the start of the Sermon on the Mount
optionC: Promises made to Israel in the Old Testament
explanation: Matthew 5:3-12 — The Beatitudes are nine blessings Jesus declared at the opening of the Sermon on the Mount.

question: How many disciples did Jesus choose?
explanation: Matthew 10:1-4 — Jesus called His twelve disciples to Him and gave them authority.

question: What did Jesus tell Peter when he walked on water and began to sink?
optionB: O you of little faith, why did you doubt
explanation: Matthew 14:31 — Jesus stretched out His hand and said to Peter, 'O you of little faith, why did you doubt?'

question: What did Jesus say is the greatest commandment?
optionC: Love the LORD your God with all your heart, soul, and mind
explanation: Matthew 22:37-38 — 'You shall love the LORD your God with all your heart, with all your soul, and with all your mind.'

question: What parable tells of a son who wasted his inheritance in a far country?
explanation: Luke 15:11-32 — The Parable of the Prodigal Son: a young man wasted his inheritance and returned repentant.

question: What did Zacchaeus do after his encounter with Jesus?
optionA: Became a disciple and followed Jesus
optionB: Gave half his goods to the poor and repaid those he had cheated fourfold
explanation: Luke 19:8 — Zacchaeus stood and said, 'Lord, I give half of my goods to the poor; if I have cheated anyone, I restore fourfold.'

question: What was the name of the pool in Jerusalem where Jesus healed a man who had been ill 38 years?
explanation: John 5:2-9 — The pool called Bethesda in Hebrew, where Jesus healed the man who had been ill for 38 years.

question: What did Jesus say about the bread He broke at the Last Supper?
explanation: Matthew 26:26 — Jesus took bread, broke it, and said 'Take, eat; this is My body.'

question: What does Jesus say about Himself in John 14:6?
optionC: I am the Way, the Truth, and the Life
optionD: I am the bread of life
explanation: John 14:6 — 'I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through Me.'

question: Who was the first disciple Jesus called in the Gospel of John?
explanation: John 1:40 — Andrew, Simon Peter's brother, was one of the two who heard John and followed Jesus.

question: What gift did Jesus promise the disciples before His ascension in Acts 1?
explanation: Acts 1:8 — 'You shall receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you.'

question: How many people were in the upper room waiting for the Holy Spirit?
explanation: Acts 1:15 — The number of disciples was about a hundred and twenty gathered together.

question: Who healed the lame man at the Beautiful Gate of the temple?
explanation: Acts 3:6-7 — Peter said 'Silver and gold I do not have... rise up and walk' and he took the man by the hand.

question: Who lied about the price of property and fell dead in Acts 5?
explanation: Acts 5:1-10 — Ananias and Sapphira lied to the Holy Spirit and both fell dead.

question: Who was the Ethiopian eunuch baptized by Philip?
optionA: A court official of the Candace, queen of the Ethiopians
explanation: Acts 8:27 — A eunuch of great authority under Candace, queen of the Ethiopians, who had charge of her treasury.

question: What was the name of the street where Ananias found Saul after his conversion?
explanation: Acts 9:11 — 'Arise and go to the street called Straight, and inquire at the house of Judas for one called Saul.'

question: Who was raised from the dead by Peter in the city of Joppa?
explanation: Acts 9:40 — Peter knelt down, prayed, and said 'Tabitha, arise.' She opened her eyes and sat up.

question: What vision did Peter have that led him to Cornelius's house?
optionA: A vision of a great city
optionB: A sheet let down from heaven with unclean animals
optionC: A man in white calling to him
explanation: Acts 10:11-12 — Peter saw a great sheet descending with all kinds of four-footed animals, reptiles, and birds.

question: Who was the first Gentile household to receive the Holy Spirit?
explanation: Acts 10:44-45 — The Holy Spirit fell on all who heard the word at Cornelius's house.

question: True or False: Ephesians 1:4 says God chose us in Christ before the foundation of the world.
explanation: Ephesians 1:4 — 'He chose us in Him before the foundation of the world, that we should be holy and without blame.'

question: According to Ephesians 5:25, how should husbands love their wives?
optionB: As Christ loved the church and gave Himself for her
explanation: Ephesians 5:25 — 'Husbands, love your wives, just as Christ also loved the church and gave Himself for her.'

question: True or False: According to 1 Timothy 2:5, there is only one mediator between God and men.
explanation: 1 Timothy 2:5 — 'For there is one God and one Mediator between God and men, the Man Christ Jesus.'

question: What does 1 Timothy 4:12 say Timothy should not let anyone do?
explanation: 1 Timothy 4:12 — 'Let no one despise your youth, but be an example to the believers.'

question: According to 2 Timothy 1:7, what spirit has God NOT given us?
explanation: 2 Timothy 1:7 — 'For God has not given us a spirit of fear, but of power and of love and of a sound mind.'

question: True or False: 2 Timothy 4:2 instructs to preach the word in season and out of season.
explanation: 2 Timothy 4:2 — 'Preach the word! Be ready in season and out of season.'

question: According to Titus 2:12, grace teaches us to live in what manner?
explanation: Titus 2:12 — 'Teaching us that... we should live soberly, righteously, and godly in the present age.'

question: True or False: James 1:5 says if anyone lacks wisdom, let him ask of God who gives liberally.
explanation: James 1:5 — 'If any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask of God, who gives to all liberally and without reproach.'

question: What does James 4:14 compare our life to?
optionB: A vapor that appears for a little time and then vanishes away
explanation: James 4:14 — 'For what is your life? It is even a vapor that appears for a little time and then vanishes away.'

question: According to 1 Peter 1:23, we are born again through what?
explanation: 1 Peter 1:23 — 'Having been born again, not of corruptible seed but incorruptible, through the word of God.'

question: True or False: 1 Peter 3:15 says to always be ready to give a defense for the hope that is in you.
explanation: 1 Peter 3:15 — 'Always be ready to give a defense to everyone who asks you a reason for the hope that is in you.'

question: According to 2 Peter 1:21, prophecy never came by what?
explanation: 2 Peter 1:21 — 'For prophecy never came by the will of man, but holy men of God spoke as they were moved by the Holy Spirit.'

question: What is the doctrine of the Second Coming?
optionB: The literal return of Jesus Christ to earth at the end of the age
optionC: The rapture of the church alone
optionD: The resurrection of the Old Testament saints
explanation: Acts 1:11 — Angels told the disciples, 'This same Jesus... will so come in like manner as you saw Him go into heaven.'

question: What is regeneration according to Christian doctrine?
optionB: The new birth — spiritual transformation by the Holy Spirit
explanation: Titus 3:5 and John 3:3-5 describe regeneration as the new birth by the Spirit that makes one a new creation.

question: What is propitiation?
optionB: The satisfaction of God's wrath through Christ's sacrifice
explanation: 1 John 2:2 — 'He Himself is the propitiation for our sins'; Jesus's death satisfied God's righteous wrath.

question: What does the doctrine of the resurrection of the dead teach?
optionB: Believers' bodies will be raised and transformed at Christ's return
optionD: The physical body is permanently destroyed
explanation: 1 Corinthians 15 and John 5:28-29 teach the bodily resurrection of the dead — both the righteous and the unrighteous.

question: What is the doctrine of eternal life?
optionB: Unending existence with God for all who believe in Christ
explanation: John 3:16 and John 17:3 — Eternal life is knowing God and Jesus Christ, a quality of life that begins now and never ends.

question: What does the word 'repentance' mean in the New Testament context?
optionB: A change of mind that leads to a change of direction
explanation: The Greek metanoia (repentance) means a radical change of mind and will that turns one from sin toward God.

question: According to Hebrews 9:22, what is required for the remission of sins?
explanation: Hebrews 9:22 — Without shedding of blood there is no remission. This principle underlies the entire sacrificial system and points to Christ's atoning work.

question: What does the Bible mean by 'election'?
optionB: God's sovereign choice of people for salvation
optionD: The selection of the twelve disciples only
explanation: Ephesians 1:4-5 — God chose us in Christ before the foundation of the world; this is the doctrine of election.

question: What two things does Romans 3:23 say about mankind?
optionA: All have loved God and all deserve blessing
optionB: All have sinned and fall short of the glory of God
optionC: All are condemned and all will die
optionD: All need baptism and all need repentance
explanation: Romans 3:23 — 'For all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God.' (cross-reference with Ephesians teaching)

question: What is the 'new covenant' according to Christian doctrine?
optionA: The covenant made with Moses on Sinai
optionB: The covenant established by Christ's blood at the Last Supper
explanation: Luke 22:20 and Hebrews 9:15 — Jesus's death established a new covenant in His blood, replacing the old Mosaic covenant.

question: What does 'redemption' mean theologically?
optionA: Buying back something by paying a price; Christ paid for our sins
explanation: Ephesians 1:7 — 'In Him we have redemption through His blood, the forgiveness of sins.'

question: According to Genesis 1:27, in whose image was man created?
explanation: Genesis 1:27 — 'So God created man in His own image; in the image of God He created him.'

question: What was the name of Adam's second son born after Abel's death?
explanation: Genesis 4:25 — Adam knew his wife again, and she bore a son and named him Seth.

question: Who was the oldest person in the Bible?
explanation: Genesis 5:27 — Methuselah lived 969 years, the longest recorded lifespan in Scripture.

question: What was the name of Noah's three sons?
explanation: Genesis 6:10 — Noah begot three sons: Shem, Ham, and Japheth.

question: What was the name of the tower the people tried to build to reach heaven?
explanation: Genesis 11:4-9 — The Tower of Babel, where God confused the language of all the earth.

question: To whom did God say 'Get out of your country... to a land that I will show you'?
explanation: Genesis 12:1 — 'Get out of your country, from your family... to a land that I will show you.' God spoke to Abram.

question: What did Jacob dream of at Bethel?
optionB: A stairway reaching to heaven with angels ascending and descending
explanation: Genesis 28:12 — Jacob dreamed of a ladder set up on the earth reaching to heaven, with angels on it.

question: What new name did God give Jacob after he wrestled with the angel?
explanation: Genesis 32:28 — 'Your name shall no longer be called Jacob, but Israel; for you have struggled with God and with men.'

question: What does the name 'Israel' mean?
optionB: Prince of God / one who struggles with God
explanation: Genesis 32:28 — 'Israel' means 'he struggles with God' or 'prince with God.'

question: What did Joseph's brothers use to fake his death to their father Jacob?
optionB: His coat dipped in goat's blood
explanation: Genesis 37:31-32 — They took Joseph's tunic, killed a goat, and dipped the tunic in blood.

question: Why did Potiphar's wife lie about Joseph?
optionB: He refused her advances and she accused him falsely
explanation: Genesis 39:12-19 — Joseph fled when Potiphar's wife grabbed him; she falsely accused him of assault.

question: What was the name of Moses's father-in-law?
explanation: Exodus 3:1 — Moses was tending the flock of Jethro his father-in-law, the priest of Midian.

question: What was in the Ark of the Covenant?
optionB: The tablets of the Law, Aaron's rod, and a jar of manna
explanation: Hebrews 9:4 — The ark contained the golden pot of manna, Aaron's rod that budded, and the tablets of the covenant.

question: What was the purpose of the bronze serpent Moses lifted in the wilderness?
optionB: Anyone who looked at it after being bitten by a serpent would live
explanation: Numbers 21:8-9 — God told Moses to make a bronze serpent; anyone bitten who looked at it would live.

question: How old was Moses when he died?
explanation: Deuteronomy 34:7 — Moses was 120 years old when he died; his eyes were not dim and his vigor had not diminished.

question: What is the first beatitude in the Sermon on the Mount?
optionA: Blessed are the pure in heart
optionC: Blessed are the poor in spirit
explanation: Matthew 5:3 — 'Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.'

question: What model prayer did Jesus give His disciples?
explanation: Matthew 6:9-13 — Jesus taught His disciples the Lord's Prayer beginning with 'Our Father in heaven.'

question: Who said 'You are the Christ, the Son of the living God'?
explanation: Matthew 16:16 — Simon Peter answered and said, 'You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.'

question: What is the Transfiguration?
optionB: Jesus being transformed in appearance on a mountain with Moses and Elijah
explanation: Matthew 17:1-3 — Jesus was transfigured on a high mountain; His face shone like the sun, and Moses and Elijah appeared.

question: In John 10, what does Jesus call Himself?
explanation: John 10:11 — 'I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd gives His life for the sheep.'

question: What did Jesus say in John 15:5 about the vine and branches?
optionB: Apart from Him we can do nothing
optionC: The branches bear fruit on their own
optionD: Cut off branches can be restored easily
explanation: John 15:5 — 'I am the vine, you are the branches... without Me you can do nothing.'

question: What was the Jewish feast being celebrated when Jesus rode into Jerusalem?
explanation: Matthew 26:2, John 12:1 — Jesus entered Jerusalem six days before the Passover.

question: How did the Holy Spirit come at Pentecost?
optionC: A rushing mighty wind and tongues of fire
explanation: Acts 2:2-3 — There came a sound like a mighty rushing wind, and divided tongues as of fire appeared on each of them.

question: Who was the sorcerer in Samaria who tried to buy the gift of the Holy Spirit?
explanation: Acts 8:9-19 — Simon the sorcerer believed and was baptized, then tried to purchase the power of the Holy Spirit.

question: What was the name of the island where Paul was shipwrecked on his way to Rome?
explanation: Acts 28:1 — After the shipwreck, they found out the island was called Malta.

question: True or False: Ephesians 6:1 instructs children to obey their parents in the Lord.
explanation: Ephesians 6:1 — 'Children, obey your parents in the Lord, for this is right.'

question: According to 1 Timothy 3:2, an overseer must be what kind of person?
optionB: Blameless, husband of one wife, temperate, sober-minded
explanation: 1 Timothy 3:2 — An overseer must be blameless, the husband of one wife, temperate, sober-minded, of good behavior.

question: What did Paul urge Timothy to fan into flame in 2 Timothy 1:6?
explanation: 2 Timothy 1:6 — 'Stir up the gift of God which is in you through the laying on of my hands.'

question: True or False: Titus 3:1 instructs believers to be subject to rulers and authorities.
optionC: Only if the ruler is a believer
optionD: Only in matters not touching faith
explanation: Titus 3:1 — 'Remind them to be subject to rulers and authorities, to obey, to be ready for every good work.'

question: According to James 5:16, what accomplishes much?
optionB: The effective, fervent prayer of a righteous man
explanation: James 5:16 — 'The effective, fervent prayer of a righteous man avails much.'

question: What does 1 Peter 5:5 instruct younger people to do in relation to elders?
optionB: Submit to elders and clothe themselves with humility
explanation: 1 Peter 5:5 — 'Likewise you younger people, submit yourselves to your elders. Clothe yourselves with humility.'

question: What are we encouraged to add to our faith in 2 Peter 1:5?
optionB: Virtue, knowledge, self-control, perseverance, godliness, brotherly kindness, love
explanation: 2 Peter 1:5-7 — Add to your faith virtue; to virtue knowledge; self-control; perseverance; godliness; brotherly kindness; love.

question: What does reconciliation mean in Christian theology?
optionB: The restoration of peace between God and sinners through Christ
optionC: Baptism uniting a believer with Christ
explanation: 2 Corinthians 5:18 — God reconciled us to Himself through Christ and gave us the ministry of reconciliation.

question: What does Scripture mean by 'grace'?
optionB: God's unmerited favor freely given to the undeserving
explanation: Ephesians 2:8-9 — Salvation is by grace through faith, not works; grace is God's free, undeserved gift.

question: What is the role of the Holy Spirit in convicting the world?
optionB: Convicting the world of sin, righteousness, and judgment
explanation: John 16:8 — 'And when He has come, He will convict the world of sin, and of righteousness, and of judgment.'
